sections.title: Reverse the Problem
points: Instead of counting members to find subsets, we start with the number of subsets.
Then we work backwards to find n, the number of members.
This uses the same formula 2ⁿ = number of subsets.

sections.title: Recognise Power of 2
points: If a set has 8 subsets, then n = 3 because 2³ = 8.
Use the table of powers to decode the answer.

sections.title: Proper Subsets to Total Subsets
points: If you are given proper subsets, add 1 to get total subsets.
Example: 15 proper subsets means 16 total subsets.

sections.title: Example with 63 Proper Subsets
points: This means the set has 6 members.

cards.fact: Find the number of set members using the number of subsets.

cards.mnemonic: Each extra item doubles the subsets.

cards.fact: All subsets including the set itself.
All subsets except the set itself.
cards.mnemonic: Proper means we remove the whole set.
